title: Public preset vocabulary violates workflow invariance
summary: Iteration-007 proposed making --preset primary and changing create.sh help/examples to manifest preset language. The audited workflow shows AI-facing skills, slash commands, root policy docs, and create.sh all currently speak in documentation levels. Public preset vocabulary would change user and AI behavior.
evidence: .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/SKILL.md tells the AI to recommend level and run create.sh --level N
recommendation: Keep --level as the only documented create.sh selection flag. Resolve level to an internal manifest contract under the hood.

title: Validator messages must not expose manifest taxonomy
summary: Iteration-007 proposed validator messages such as manifest contract, active manifest profiles, and choose a lighter manifest preset. Validator output is AI-visible and often user-visible during completion gates, so those messages break the invariant.
evidence: .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/010-template-levels/002-template-greenfield-redesign/research/iterations/iteration-007.md proposed check-files.sh and check-section-counts.sh messages with manifest/preset language
Current .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/rules/check-files.sh says All required files present for Level $level
recommendation: Map internal resolver failures and validation summaries back to Level $level or taxonomy-neutral file/section messages.

title: Level remains the public API; only level folders disappear internally
summary: Iteration 9 said levels disappear. Under the workflow-invariant constraint, that statement is too strong. Levels must remain in AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, SKILL.md, command markdown, create.sh help/stdout/JSON, authored docs, and parser metadata.
evidence: AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md document Level 1/2/3/3+ and phase parent as the operator contract
system-spec-kit SKILL.md requires determining level before file changes
Templates and parser health code rely on SPECKIT_LEVEL
recommendation: Revise the synthesis to say duplicated level folders disappear as source implementation, while levels remain the public and AI-facing contract.

title: Preset names should be 1:1 level aliases
summary: Semantic names like simple-change, validated-change, arch-change, and governed-change create a second taxonomy. Even if intended as internal, they risk leaking through logs, errors, graph metadata, and future docs.
evidence: research.md iteration-9 synthesis lists user-facing presets as simple-change, validated-change, arch-change, governed-change, phase-parent
Iteration-007 helper errors include Unknown preset and Ambiguous preset
recommendation: Use internal names level-1, level-2, level-3, level-3-plus, and phase-parent, or hide preset terminology entirely behind resolve_level_contract(level).

title: SPECKIT_LEVEL and spec_level must be preserved
summary: Current templates, fixtures, and parser health checks rely on SPECKIT_LEVEL and spec_level. Replacing them with capability declarations would break backward readability and alter AI-authored packet expectations.
evidence: .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/shared/parsing/spec-doc-health.ts parses SPECKIT_LEVEL and warns when absent
Template and test fixtures contain SPECKIT_LEVEL markers and Level table rows
recommendation: Keep SPECKIT_LEVEL, Level table rows, level frontmatter, and spec_level metadata. Derive internal manifest contracts from these public markers.

title: graph-metadata contract persistence needs an invariant-safe ADR
summary: Iteration 9 recommended writing resolved template_contract into graph-metadata.json. If that object contains preset/kind/capabilities, it is visible in a file the AI may read during resume and may leak into prompts or summaries.
evidence: resume.md explicitly reads graph-metadata.json after canonical docs for packet dependencies and hints
recommendation: ADR-005 should decide whether to derive from SPECKIT_LEVEL at runtime or persist only a level-shaped public contract without preset/kind/capabilities.

summary: Change the C+F recommendation from public preset migration to level-invariant internal manifest resolution.
before: replace primary --level with --preset; keep --level as legacy alias
after: keep --level as primary and documented; internal resolver maps level to manifest contract

summary: Change preset catalog names to level-preserving aliases.

before: frontmatter contract could replace preserved SPECKIT_LEVEL semantics
after: SPECKIT_LEVEL, Level table rows, level frontmatter, and spec_level stay canonical public markers
